rag_project_experiment.title: Merdrake Root Trial / Meileb 1977-09-11
rag_project_experiment.one_line: Caleb, Mei, and Sprout are testing whether very young Mandrake seedlings can accept water as a home and grow in forcing glasses, creating a possible 'merdrake' water-root variant; current trial has dark, slime-light, and clean-light arms.

narrative_function.why_it_matters: Turns Caleb's curiosity about plant properties into a live Herbology experiment under Sprout's supervision. Shows Mei's methodological discipline and Sprout's living-plant lens on Caleb's broader potion/herb work.

known_canon.item: Mandrakes are magical plants with powerful restorative properties.
Mandrake roots are humanoid/baby-like when young and mature over several months.
Mandrake cries are dangerous: full-grown cries can kill; baby cries can knock people out for hours.
Mandrake is used in Mandrake Restorative Draught, including for reversing Petrification / restoring cursed or Transfigured people.
Canon/natural baseline places Mandrakes in soil; Hogwarts students repot them in Herbology.

source_notes.item: Harry Potter Lexicon summarizes Chamber of Secrets Mandrake facts: restorative properties, baby-to-adult maturation, fatal adult cries, baby cries causing unconsciousness, and use in Mandrake Restorative Draught.
Harry Potter Wiki lists Mandrake's natural environment as soil and notes humanoid root behavior and restorative-draught use.

limits.item: Canon does not establish whether Mandrakes can live long-term in water.
Canon does not establish whether a water-grown Mandrake would have altered magical properties.
Merdrake is campaign invention, not canon Mandrake lore.

in_world_hypothesis.question: Can very young Mandrake seedlings accept water as a home and continue growing without soil, and if so does that produce a meaningful water-root / 'merdrake' condition?
in_world_hypothesis.logic: Mandrakes scream when uprooted as protest at being torn from home, but the seedlings settled when their roots touched water. Sprout treats this as a real first datum: water may have been accepted as home, though quiet does not prove thriving.

normal_conditions.item: Mandrakes normally live in soil / pots.
Repotting or disturbance triggers dangerous cries.
A new pot that the plant accepts quiets the Mandrake.
Young Mandrakes mature over months before harvest/use.

changed_conditions.item: Roots suspended in clean water instead of soil.
Crowns seated above water by collars/twine.
Clear forcing glasses expose roots to visible monitoring.
Algae either allowed to develop or removed by Evanesco in a separate arm.

helpers_or_witnesses.item: Sprout's greenhouse context; Liri not present during current checks

materials_and_setup.location: Sprout's greenhouse / Greenhouse Three, rear bench

materials.item: Hyacinth-style clear forcing glasses, waisted, big enough for young Mandrake roots
Collar/twine to seat crown above water
Youngest Mandrake seedlings selected from Sprout's stock
Evanesco for algae removal in glass 3 only

safety.item: Earmuffs/muffs always for handling and careful checks.
Silencing Charm protects against future disturbance; do not rely on silence as proof of no danger.
Avoid unnecessary disturbance, bench knocks, or careless tarp lifting.
Dark glass must not be opened in daylight.

item.name: Dark / tarped control
item.condition: Young Mandrake seedling in water, kept in darkness under tarp.

protocol.item: Keep tarped except for evening minimal-light checks.
Check after sundown, roughly 19:00 or later; full dark is better.
Use muffs; one careful lift; log the looking, not just the growing.

observations.item: Initially settled when root touched water.
Not checked during Tuesday 14:18 daylight because that would contaminate the dark variable.

item.name: Light / ungroomed slime arm
item.condition: Young Mandrake seedling in water, bare to light, algae allowed to develop.

protocol.item: Do not clean algae; algae is now part of the experiment.
Let it answer whether light-bred slime harms, chokes, or is resisted by the root.

observations.item: At ~26 hours: seedling settled, quiet, alive; first algae bloom began.
At ~51 hours: seedling visibly growing; pale root had first hint of form; water properly green with algae blooming up walls/clouding view.

item.name: Light / kept-clean arm
item.condition: Young Mandrake seedling in water, bare to light, algae removed without algaecide.

protocol.item: Evanesce slime itself, not the water, every 1–2 days.
No algaecide: treatment in water would add a variable.

observations.item: Started one day later than original pair; delay considered negligible at long endpoint.
At Tuesday 14:18: clearer than glass 2, root one stage behind, faint green haze starting at waterline.
At Tuesday 14:19: Caleb thoroughly Evanesced algae haze and film; water bright, root sharp, clean-light arm reset.

item.result: Sprout supplies matched pair of clear waisted forcing glasses; prep required scalding, Silencing Charm, and collar to hold crown above water.
Two youngest Mandrake seedlings placed roots-down in water: left tarped/dark, right bare/full light. Both settled when root touched water.
Scream is protest at uprooting/disturbance, not a constant state. Water was accepted enough for the seedlings to hush; quiet is not proof of thriving.
Bare glass alive after ~26h with algae beginning; dark glass held for evening to avoid daylight contamination.

item.event: Cleaning idea rejected for original pair
item.result: Sprout and Mei explain cleaning/algaecide would introduce confounds; algae in glass 2 becomes part of experiment.
Glass 3 becomes clean-light arm: bare to light, algae removed every 1–2 days by Evanesco, no algaecide.
Glass 2 green/slimy but growing; glass 3 clearer and one stage behind; glass 1 left tarped because afternoon light would contaminate dark variable.
Caleb Evanesced algae haze/film from glass 3; water bright; no algaecide, water untouched.
Glass 1 should be checked only after roughly 19:00 or later; glass 2 and 3 may be checked any time.

checking_rules.item: Glass 1: check only after sundown, roughly 19:00+; full dark preferred; muffs on; minimal lift.
Glass 2: check whenever; do not clean; log algae and growth.
Glass 3: check whenever; remove slime/film with Evanesco every 1–2 days; log misses.
Always log the looking/checking, not just plant condition.

maintenance_rules.item: Do not add algaecide to the research arms.
Do not Scourgify the water or change water unless a new experimental arm is explicitly created.
Glass 3 maintenance targets slime/film only, not water itself.

do_not_do.item: Do not treat a 20-second daylight peek at glass 1 as harmless; it is stray-light contamination.
Do not say the seedlings are constantly screaming under the Silencing Charm.
Do not claim quiet means thriving.
Do not collapse glass 2 and glass 3 into one light condition; one is slime arm, one is clean-light arm.

open_questions.item: Can Mandrakes live long-term in water?
Does water-grown Mandrake root develop different magical properties?
Does darkness improve water-root survival by suppressing algae or by some magical plant-response?
Does light itself harm/help the water-root, independent of algae?
Does algae kill the bare light arm or become tolerated/resisted?
Can a stable 'merdrake root' be grown for potions later?

continuity_guardrails.item: Merdrake is a campaign research term, not canon Mandrake lore.
Initial pair started Sunday 11 Sept ~11:00; glass 3 started Monday 12 Sept ~13:28.
Glass 1 = dark/tarped; glass 2 = light/ungroomed/slime; glass 3 = light/cleaned.
Current active pending item is evening glass-1 dark check after ~19:00.
Sprout is supervising/allowing it, not losing control of her greenhouse.
Mei logs precisely and protects methodology; Caleb pushes curiosity; Sprout frames living-plant logic.

item.facts: Sprout supplies matched hyacinth-style forcing glasses, with preparation: scald, Silencing Charm, collar at neck to seat crown above water.

Experiment begins: two young mandrake seedlings, roots in clean water, left tarped/dark, right bare/light, muffs used, Mei Silences glasses, label DANGER! MERDRAKE ROOT. Meileb 1977-09-11. Sprout explains scream as protest, not constant state; water accepted as home but quiet does not prove thriving.
First check after ~26 hours: bare glass alive, settled, quiet; first algae bloom; dark glass held for evening.
Sprout and Mei reject cleaning original pair because it confounds light-vs-cleaning; algae becomes part of the experiment.
Glass #3 added as bare-to-light but kept algae-free arm; algaecide rejected; Evanesce slime itself every 1–2 days, not the water; started one day later.
Day-2 check: Glass 2 visibly growing but green/slimy; Glass 3 clearer and a stage behind; Glass 1 left tarped because afternoon light would contaminate dark control; Glass 3 Evanesced thoroughly.
Clear rule: Glass 1 only after sundown, roughly 19:00 or later; Glasses 2 and 3 can be checked any time.

facts.item: Mandrake root matures from baby-like root to adult-like humanoid form over months.
Adult Mandrake cries can kill; baby cries can knock a person out for hours.
Mandrake Restorative Draught returns Transfigured or cursed people to their original state / treats Petrification.
Roots are humanoid in shape and behaviour.
Cries are fatal when mature; seedlings are dangerous but less lethal.
Stewed Mandrake is used in Mandrake Restorative Draught.
